terms.definition: Text enclosed in double quotes that creates a String instance at compile time.
Joining strings (and other values) into a single string; evaluated left-to-right.
Returns the number of char units in the string.
Returns the char at a zero-based index.
Returns the half-open slice [start, end); end is exclusive.
Returns the first index where s occurs, or -1 if not found.
Returns the last index where s occurs, or -1 if not found.
Checks content equality; use for comparing string values.
Checks content equality ignoring letter case.
Lexicographically compares two strings; negative, zero, or positive.
Return new strings with all letters converted to upper/lower case.
Returns a new string with all occurrences of old replaced by new.
A mutable sequence of characters for efficient appending and editing.
terms.example: StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder(); sb.append("hi"); String s = sb.toString();

terms.definition: Creates formatted strings using printf-style placeholders.
